steps.title: Identify what makes an effective contrast
steps.content: The student explicitly wants to “contrast” the two types. An effective contrast must show a SPECIFIC difference between first-class and second-class levers using precise details from the notes—not general definitions that apply to all levers.

steps.title: Find the choice that delivers the specific contrast
steps.content:
The matching choice directly states where the fulcrum and load are positioned relative to the effort in each type, showing the precise difference between them.

The others either define general lever terms (A), vaguely acknowledge different locations without specifics (B), or emphasize what both types share instead of how they differ (C).

D is correct.
